items.title: Member Support Discovery discovery
items.name: Member Support Discovery discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether members need stronger topic guidance, better recommendation fit, or clearer live-session entry points to convert faster.
items.opportunity: Clarify which support entry patterns most improve first-session usefulness and short-term retention.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to improve member intent routing, recommendation quality, and first-session conversion into meaningful support.

riskFocus.question: Will members act more often when Cadre turns vague needs into stronger next-step guidance?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Members convert faster when the first screen frames support around relatable topics and one high-confidence next action.

plannedActivities: Compare first-session abandonment by intent type.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which support-entry pattern becomes the default for high-need first sessions in Q2.

items.title: Employer Launch and Reporting discovery
items.name: Employer Launch and Reporting discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether launch underperformance comes more from access setup, weak launch comms, or unclear admin reporting.
items.opportunity: Clarify which launch and reporting changes most improve first-quarter activation for employer accounts.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to reduce employer launch friction, improve admin workflows, and make aggregate account-health reporting more actionable.

riskFocus.question: Do employer buyers renew because the program launches faster and shows clearer aggregate value?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Faster launches and clearer aggregate reporting increase buyer confidence enough to improve renewal and expansion decisions.

plannedActivities: Map launch delays by step and account type.
Review employer questions raised in quarterly reviews.
Test revised admin summaries and activation benchmarks.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which employer setup and reporting changes become the default onboarding pattern in Q2.

items.title: Contributor Trust and Delivery discovery
items.name: Contributor Trust and Delivery discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing where contributors lose confidence between onboarding, session hosting, and payout settlement.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether contributor churn is driven more by unclear profile setup, live delivery friction, or payout legibility.

linkedInitiatives.description: Focused investment to improve contributor profile quality, live session operations, moderation support, and payout clarity.

riskFocus.question: Can contributors reliably operate live and recorded experiences without needing manual rescue?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Contributor trust increases materially when setup, moderation, and payouts feel predictable enough to repeat regularly.

plannedActivities: Analyze live-session failure and rescue patterns.
Test clearer payout and earnings explanations.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which contributor workflow improvements become standard before broader supply expansion.
